data.content:
Found the issue. The target generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` into the `obj/` directory, but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to include the generated file in compilation via `<Compile Include="..." />` inside the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation so the C# compiler sees it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but MSBuild doesn't know to include it in compilation.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` after writing the file in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation so the C# compiler sees it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation so the C# compiler sees it -->
    <ItemGroup>

    </ItemGroup>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the `obj/` intermediate directory but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild only compiles files it knows about — files outside the project directory (like those in `obj/`) aren't auto-included.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is fed to the C# compiler.
